gates.title: Customer Value
gates.description: Validate that the AI product solves a real customer problem.

questions.prompt: Has the customer problem been clearly defined?
questions.help_text: The team should be able to explain the user pain point in one sentence.

questions.prompt: Has the target persona been identified?
questions.help_text: The product should be designed for a specific user segment.

questions.prompt: Have success metrics been defined?
questions.help_text: Examples include adoption, completion rate, CSAT, or reduction in support contacts.

questions.prompt: Has the customer journey been documented?
questions.help_text: The team should understand where the AI experience fits in the broader journey.

gates.title: AI Quality
gates.description: Assess whether the AI experience is accurate, useful, and ready for launch.

questions.prompt: Has a human evaluation been completed?
questions.help_text: A human review should validate the AI experience on realistic scenarios.

questions.prompt: Is there a golden dataset or evaluation set?
questions.help_text: The team should have representative examples to test quality consistently.

questions.prompt: Has the hallucination threshold been reviewed?
questions.help_text: The team should define what level of incorrect output is acceptable.

questions.prompt: Has policy compliance been validated?
questions.help_text: The AI should follow business rules and product policy correctly.

questions.prompt: Has escalation behavior been reviewed for high-risk cases?
questions.help_text: The system should know when to hand off to a human.

gates.title: Trust & Safety
gates.description: Review Responsible AI risks and customer trust considerations.

questions.prompt: Has privacy review been completed?
questions.help_text: The team should verify that data handling and usage are appropriate.

questions.prompt: Has a bias or fairness review been completed?
questions.help_text: The team should check for uneven behavior across user groups or scenarios.

questions.prompt: Has prompt injection or adversarial testing been completed?
questions.help_text: The system should be tested against attempts to manipulate behavior.

questions.prompt: Has a security review been completed?
questions.help_text: The feature should be reviewed for leakage, misuse, or unsafe access patterns.

gates.title: Operational Readiness
gates.description: Ensure the organization can monitor, support, and recover from issues.

questions.prompt: Are monitoring dashboards configured?
questions.help_text: The team should be able to observe system health and quality after launch.

questions.prompt: Are alerting thresholds defined?
questions.help_text: The team should know when to investigate or intervene.

questions.prompt: Is the support team trained?
questions.help_text: Support should understand what the feature does and how to respond to issues.

questions.prompt: Is a rollback plan documented?
questions.help_text: The team should be able to safely disable or revert the feature if needed.

gates.title: Business Readiness
gates.description: Confirm the feature is aligned with business goals and rollout strategy.

questions.prompt: Has expected ROI been estimated?
questions.help_text: The team should know the business value expected from the feature.

questions.prompt: Has an executive sponsor been identified?
questions.help_text: A sponsor helps align decisions and unblock cross-functional issues.

questions.prompt: Has the rollout strategy been approved?
questions.help_text: The team should know whether the launch is beta, phased, or full rollout.
